figsimR: Simulating Fig Wasp Community Assembly

Source: R/figsimR-package.R
figsimR-package.Rd

Simulates the assembly and structure of fig wasp communities within fig fruits (Ficus racemosa), capturing ecological processes such as entry order, parasitism, spatial oviposition constraints, resource limitation, and fruit abortion. Designed for ecological modeling and hypothesis testing using empirical parameters. Current version 0.2.1 is integrated with the "sink strength" theory. Added support for species-level `none` entries in `layer_preference`, allowing some species to bypass the ovary-layer preference filter. Clarified that `layer_preference` refers to ovary-layer preference or accessibility. Improved handling of user-defined fig-wasp communities or other species interaction systems with custom species lists and species-specific parameter objects. Retained *Ficus racemosa*-specific defaults for the worked example while allowing users to override them with system-specific parameters.

See also
